metadata.title: Russia as energy, nuclear-fuel & Arctic chokepoint — shadow fleet, Rosatom/HALEU, and the Northern Sea Route
metadata.status: facts from CREA/EU/Rosatom/major-press; the 'sanctions forged a China-India energy axis' framing is labeled interpretation
metadata.frame: The adversary the corpus underweighted: Russia holds three chokepoints the AI/defense economy touches - OIL/GAS (now routed to China/India via a sanctioned shadow fleet), NUCLEAR FUEL (Rosatom ~44% of global enrichment + the HALEU bottleneck for the SMRs the AI-power build needs), and the ARCTIC (Rosatom controls the Northern Sea Route + the only nuclear icebreaker fleet). Sanctions didn't close these - they re-routed them into a China/India axis. Overlay; excluded from the proofs. Connects to energy_web, power_adequacy (HALEU P2 UNSAT), geopolitics-allied-intel-nodes (Arctic), geopolitics-contested-resource-states (Iran shadow-fleet parallel), macro-ai-power-grid-bottleneck.

oil_gas_shadow_fleet.the_fleet: Russia sustains war revenue via a 'shadow fleet' of unregistered/uninsured tankers selling discounted crude. Jan 2026: shadow-fleet exports ~14.32M tonnes of crude, CHINA the largest recipient while INDIA cut volumes sharply (US-India trade pressure). China took ~33.8% of all Russian exports in 2024 (~75% oil & gas). status: fact.
oil_gas_shadow_fleet.lng_caution: India refused Russian LNG from the sanctioned Portovaya (Baltic) plant; Arctic LNG 2 is also sanctioned but Russia reopened its eastern NSR export corridor (the icebreaking carrier Christophe de Margerie ran the route in ~13 days, from 26 May). status: fact.
oil_gas_shadow_fleet.sanctions: EU 19th package (23 Oct 2025) targeted Russian energy, third-country banks, and crypto providers; 2026 US action tightened the shadow-fleet net. The fleet adapts; the revenue persists. status: fact.
oil_gas_shadow_fleet.read: sanctions converted Russia's energy from a Western market into a discounted China/India supply - the same shadow-fleet mechanism as Iran (geopolitics-contested-resource-states); the barrels still flow, just eastward and cheaper.

nuclear_fuel_rosatom.haleu_chokepoint: Russia (Rosatom/Tenex) is ~44% of global uranium ENRICHMENT and the dominant source of HALEU - the high-assay low-enriched fuel nearly every Western advanced/SMR reactor design needs. The corpus's power_adequacy P2 proves US fuel independence infeasible to ~2029; Centrus (Piketon) is the thin domestic response. status: fact.
nuclear_fuel_rosatom.leverage: Rosatom is largely UN-sanctioned (Western reactors depend on its fuel), giving Russia a quiet chokepoint over the nuclear leg of the AI-power build (the SMR/nuclear-restart wave at Three Mile Island etc.). status: fact + interpretation.
nuclear_fuel_rosatom.read: the AI build's 'go nuclear for clean firm power' answer runs into a Russian fuel chokepoint - money can't buy past it on the datacenter timeline.

arctic.nsr_control: Rosatom has near-total control of the Northern Sea Route and operates the world's only fleet of nuclear-powered ICEBREAKERS. Oct 2025: Rosatom + China's NDRC signed an NSR action plan; Russia invited India to build (non-nuclear) icebreakers and expand NSR LNG logistics to Indian ports. status: fact.
arctic.shadow_fleet_arctic: The NSR is becoming a shadow-fleet corridor: ~100 sanctioned 'dark fleet' vessels used it in 2025, up from ~13 in 2024 - a ~7x jump. status: fact.
arctic.militarization: Russia rebuilt Kola Peninsula / Novaya Zemlya bases + a modernized submarine fleet; NATO responded with Arctic Sentry (geopolitics-allied-intel-nodes). The Arctic fuses energy + fuel + shipping + military + the GIUK/undersea-cable layer. status: fact.

the_pattern: Russia is the chokepoint holder the AI-capex story rarely names: it gates NUCLEAR FUEL (HALEU - the clean-firm-power answer), holds an ARCTIC route + icebreaker monopoly, and keeps its energy revenue flowing via a shadow fleet now serving China/India. Sanctions did not remove these levers; they reorganized the buyers. Combined with China's materials leverage (geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war) and the Iran oil contest, it completes the adversary side of the physical-chokepoint map: the West controls equipment + capital; the adversaries control fuel, materials, and routes.
